growth_records.strain: MS941 dsrS (MS941 carrying plasmid pMM1520dsrS encoding dextransucrase DsrS from Leuconostoc mesenteroides NRRL B-512F)
growth_records.medium: Defined glucose batch medium (optimized for B. megaterium growth)

up.method: time-course of glucose concentration shown in Figure 4A (batch, on-line/off-line assay per Hollmann & Deckwer 2004); no numeric consumption rate given in text

sec.compound: dextransucrase (heterologous glucosyltransferase DsrS, recombinant protein product)
sec.units: mg/gCDW/h (approx; 6.75 mg dextransucrase per g CDW formed during the first hour after induction, the hour of maximal production)
sec.method: SDS-PAGE activity-staining quantification of secreted/cell-associated DsrS; rate approximated as 6.75 mg/gCDW over 1 h
measured in culture medium; reported qualitatively as 'very low concentration' (PTA/overflow strongly down-regulated; acetyl-CoA channeled to PHB instead)

product_yields.dextransucrase: ~2% of total protein synthesized during first hour post-induction; 6.75 mg DsrS/gCDW vs biomass increase 750 mg/L in that hour

growth_records.cite: Wang W et al. Proteome analysis of a recombinant Bacillus megaterium strain during heterologous production of a glucosyltransferase. Proteome Science (2005). DOI:10.1186/1477-5956-3-4. [PMC1175100]
growth_records.snippet: It contains per liter 33 g glucose monohydate, 5.0 g (NH4)2SO4, 2.2 g KH2PO4, 0.3 g MgSO4.7H2O, 0.5 g yeast extract, 2.0 mL trace element solution ... batch culture was carried out at 37C, pH 5.2, 500 rpm and aeration of 1 L air/min ... 6.7
growth_records.notes: Defined-medium aerobic batch of a prokaryote (B. megaterium MS941 dsrS) with full medium composition stated (Materials and methods). Conditions: 37C, pH 5.2, 500 rpm, 1 L air/min => aerobic. Growth rate NOT numerically derivable: text gives biomass increase of 750 mg/L within the first hour post-induction but no absolute biomass value at that time, and OD578 time-course only shown graphically (Fig 4A); therefore growth_rate_per_h left null. Product secretion: '6.75 mg dextransucrase/g CDW were built' during the first hour of maximal production => rate approx 6.75 mg/gCDW/h (heterologous protein, not a central-metabolite exchange). Acetate reported only qualitatively as 'very low' (no number) because PTA and PDH were down-regulated and acetyl-CoA was diverted to polyhydroxybutyrate (PHB, up to 32% CDW cited from ref 19). Xylose inducer (0.5% w/v = 5 g/L) not consumed due to glucose catabolite repression. Preculture: 50 mL medium, 37C, 250 rpm overnight to OD578~3, then 10 mL into 990 mL. CDW computed from OD578 via an unpublished linear OD-CDW relation. No 13C-MFA performed (proteomics + preliminary enzyme activity only).

up.method: 1H NMR of extracellular substrate depletion combined with biomass growth rate, regression analysis; absolute value in Supplementary Table S1/S2 (not in article body)

sec.method: LC-MS of extracellular metabolite, regression analysis; gluconate secretion evident during growth on glucose (Supplementary Table S1); absolute value not in article body

mfa.note: All fluxes normalized to glucose uptake = 100%; absolute fluxes in Supplementary Table S2 (13CFLUX2 MFA), OD600=2, n=2 biological replicates

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: G6P -> F6P (EMP entry) vs G6P -> 6P-gluconate -> oxidative PP pathway
mfa.note: Abstract/Results: equal partition (~50:50) of consumed glucose from G6P into EMP pathway and into PP pathway via 6P-gluconate for glucose-grown cells
Active gluconate pathway confirmed by 13C; assimilation from G6P to 6P-gluconate favored over gluconate route (6P-gluconate >60% 13C-labeled in glucose:gluconate feed)
ED pathway inactive/absent in B. megaterium QM B1551 (non-labeled pyruvate and DHAP <5%)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: oxidative PP pathway contribution to R5P pool
mfa.note: ~72% of R5P synthesized from 6P-gluconate via oxidative PP pathway (glucose:gluconate feed, ~20% R5P non-labeled)
77% R5P and 72% Xu5P singly 13C-labeled (oxidative PP), only up to 18% triply-labeled (non-oxidative), on [1,2-13C2]-glucose

yields.Yxs_gCDW_per_OD600: 0.61 +/- 0.08 gCDW/L per OD600 (glucose growth); conversion factor, not a substrate yield

growth_records.cite: Wushensky JA et al. Flux Connections Between Gluconate Pathway, Glycolysis, and Pentose-Phosphate Pathway During Carbohydrate Metabolism in Bacillus megaterium QM B1551. Frontiers in Microbiology (2018). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2018.02789. [PMC6262346]
growth_records.snippet: Quantitative flux analysis of glucose-grown cells showed equal partition of consumed glucose from G6P to the Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas (EMP) pathway and from G6P to the PP pathway through 6P-gluconate.
growth_records.notes: 13C-MFA paper (13CFLUX2). Glucose-alone condition. Absolute uptake/secretion/growth rates were MEASURED (1H NMR substrate depletion in mmol gCDW-1 h-1; LC-MS excretion in umol gCDW-1 h-1; OD600 + gCDW biomass) but the numeric values reside in Supplementary Tables S1/S2 and Figure 6, which are not present in the fulltext XML; only relative fluxes normalized to glucose uptake=100% appear in the body, captured above. Biomass conversion factor 0.61+/-0.08 gCDW L-1 per OD600 for glucose. Culture 30C, pH 7.0, 220 rpm, aerobic batch. No mu value stated in text.

growth_records.medium: Defined minimal medium (equimolar carbon-equivalent glucose:fructose:xylose mixture)

up.method: 13C-MFA; fructose->F6P uptake flux about half the glucose uptake flux; second annotated route (fructose->FBP) inactive
13C-MFA; no assimilatory flux of xylose-derived carbons detected

sec.method: LC-MS; gluconate excretion (blue flux in Fig 6B); absolute value in Supplementary Table S2 (not in article body)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: gluconate pathway flux (mixture vs glucose alone)
mfa.note: ~two-fold increase of flux through gluconate pathway on the mixture vs glucose alone
carbon flux from G6P to F6P decreased by up to 12% on mixture vs glucose alone
17% increase in oxidative PP activity on mixture vs glucose alone
about half of glucose uptake flux; enters EMP at F6P node
increased by up to 70% due to fructose-derived carbon influx at F6P
no assimilation of xylose-derived carbons during simultaneous feeding

yields.Yxs_gCDW_per_OD600: 0.43 +/- 0.10 gCDW/L per OD600 (glucose:fructose:xylose); conversion factor, not a substrate yield

growth_records.cite: Wushensky JA et al. Flux Connections Between Gluconate Pathway, Glycolysis, and Pentose-Phosphate Pathway During Carbohydrate Metabolism in Bacillus megaterium QM B1551. Frontiers in Microbiology (2018). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2018.02789. [PMC6262346]
growth_records.snippet: During growth on the mixture, the uptake flux from fructose to F6P was about half of the glucose uptake flux... there was no assimilatory flux of xylose-derived carbons.
growth_records.notes: 13C-MFA on the ternary carbohydrate mixture (glucose:fructose:xylose, equimolar carbon-equivalent, 330 mM C total). All MFA fluxes normalized to glucose uptake=100%; absolute fluxes in Supplementary Table S2 (not in body). Fructose uptake=~50% and xylose=0% are model-derived relative fluxes (is_experimental:false); glucose uptake and gluconate secretion were experimentally constrained. Biomass conversion factor 0.43+/-0.10 gCDW L-1 per OD600. 30C, pH 7.0, 220 rpm, aerobic batch.

growth_records.medium: Defined minimal medium (1:1 glucose:gluconate mixture, 330 mM C total)

up.method: 1H NMR substrate depletion; absolute value in Supplementary Table S1 (not in body)
co-fed with glucose; non-labeled 6P-gluconate fraction rose 18%->36% across T1->T2 confirming active gluconate uptake pathway

mfa.note: increase from 18% to 36% confirms active gluconate pathway; but >60% 13C-labeled shows glucose->G6P->6P-gluconate route favored over gluconate route
16-fold reduction of 6P-gluconate pool on glucose:gluconate, indicating limited flux from gluconate to 6P-gluconate
~18-fold FBP accumulation; FBP production from F6P exceeds FBP lysis when gluconate co-present

growth_records.cite: Wushensky JA et al. Flux Connections Between Gluconate Pathway, Glycolysis, and Pentose-Phosphate Pathway During Carbohydrate Metabolism in Bacillus megaterium QM B1551. Frontiers in Microbiology (2018). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2018.02789. [PMC6262346]
growth_records.snippet: Across the two timepoints during exponential growth on this glucose:gluconate mixture, the fraction of non-labeled 6P-gluconate increased from 18% to 36%, confirming the presence of an active gluconate pathway.
growth_records.notes: 1:1 glucose:gluconate co-feeding condition, 330 mM C total (same total carbon as glucose-alone). Used to prove gluconate pathway activity; intracellular metabolite pool ratios and labeling reported (values above are pool fold-changes vs glucose-alone from Fig 2C, not exchange fluxes). Absolute uptake/growth rates not in article body. Biomass conversion factor 0.53+/-0.07 gCDW L-1 per OD600. 30C, pH 7.0, aerobic batch.

growth_records.medium: Defined minimal medium (fructose alone, 330 mM C)

up.method: growth on fructose as sole carbon source obtained (Supplementary Figure S1); no numeric rate in article body

growth_records.cite: Wushensky JA et al. Flux Connections Between Gluconate Pathway, Glycolysis, and Pentose-Phosphate Pathway During Carbohydrate Metabolism in Bacillus megaterium QM B1551. Frontiers in Microbiology (2018). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2018.02789. [PMC6262346]
growth_records.snippet: we were able to obtain growth of the B. megaterium cells when the sole carbon source was given as fructose (another common hexose) or xylose (a common pentose) (Supplementary Figure S1).
growth_records.notes: Fructose-alone growth confirmed qualitatively; growth curve in Supplementary Figure S1 (not in fulltext XML). No numeric growth/uptake rate in article body. 30C, pH 7.0, 220 rpm, aerobic batch, 330 mM C fructose.

growth_records.medium: Defined minimal medium (xylose alone, 330 mM C)

up.method: growth on xylose as sole carbon source obtained (Supplementary Figure S1); no numeric rate in article body

growth_records.cite: Wushensky JA et al. Flux Connections Between Gluconate Pathway, Glycolysis, and Pentose-Phosphate Pathway During Carbohydrate Metabolism in Bacillus megaterium QM B1551. Frontiers in Microbiology (2018). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2018.02789. [PMC6262346]
growth_records.snippet: we were able to obtain growth of the B. megaterium cells when the sole carbon source was given as ... xylose (a common pentose) (Supplementary Figure S1).
growth_records.notes: Xylose-alone growth confirmed qualitatively (Supplementary Figure S1, not in fulltext XML). Note: during the ternary mixture there was NO assimilatory xylose flux, but xylose can support growth as sole carbon source. No numeric rate in body. 30C, pH 7.0, 220 rpm, aerobic batch, 330 mM C xylose.

up.method: calculated from experimental mu, glucose conc, biomass, time via Eq.(1)

growth_records.cite: Aminian-Dehkordi J et al. Manually curated genome-scale reconstruction of the metabolic network of Bacillus megaterium DSM319. Scientific Reports (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41598-019-55041-w. [PMC6904757]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2: DSM319 specific growth rate 0.106 1/h; Glucose uptake rate 1.52 mmol/gBM/h; Acetate uptake rate 0.15 mmol/gBM/h. 'All strains were cultivated in M9 medium with glucose as the sole carbon source, under aerobic chemostat conditions.'
growth_records.notes: Values from Table 2 ('Simulation conditions for different B. m. DSM319 strains'). Experimental specific growth rate 0.106/h cited from ref 37 (Biedendieck et al.). Glucose uptake 1.52 mmol/gBM/h computed from experimental data via Eq.(1); acetate uptake 0.15 mmol/gBM/h. doubling_time=ln2/mu=0.693/0.106=6.54 h. A separate 13C-MFA (Fig.10, ref 37, [U-13C]glucose) is discussed but numeric net fluxes appear only in the figure image (normalized to glucose uptake %), not extractable from text; Table 3 lists the reactions but no flux values.

up.method: calculated via Eq.(1) from experimental data

growth_records.cite: Aminian-Dehkordi J et al. Manually curated genome-scale reconstruction of the metabolic network of Bacillus megaterium DSM319. Scientific Reports (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41598-019-55041-w. [PMC6904757]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2: MS941 specific growth rate 0.11 1/h; Glucose uptake 1.62 mmol/gBM/h; Acetate uptake 0.17 mmol/gBM/h.
growth_records.notes: Table 2. MS941 = DSM319 delta-nprM derivative; mutation reported not to remarkably influence glucose-based growth. doubling_time=0.693/0.11=6.30 h. Uptake fluxes derived via Eq.(1).

up.method: calculated via Eq.(1) from high-cell-density experimental data (Wang et al.)

growth_records.cite: Aminian-Dehkordi J et al. Manually curated genome-scale reconstruction of the metabolic network of Bacillus megaterium DSM319. Scientific Reports (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41598-019-55041-w. [PMC6904757]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2 (MS941, second column): specific growth rate 0.426 1/h; Glucose uptake 5.17 mmol/gBM/h; Acetate uptake 0.6 mmol/gBM/h. 'applying the results reported by Wang et al. to the analysis of MS941 and WH320 strains under high cell density
growth_records.notes: Table 2 MS941 second data column corresponds to high-cell-density condition (mu 0.426/h, much higher glucose uptake 5.17). Experimental data from Wang et al. (ref 16). doubling_time=0.693/0.426=1.63 h. Culture mode inferred fed-batch from 'high cell density' wording (not explicitly named).

up.method: calculated via Eq.(1) from experimental data

growth_records.cite: Aminian-Dehkordi J et al. Manually curated genome-scale reconstruction of the metabolic network of Bacillus megaterium DSM319. Scientific Reports (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41598-019-55041-w. [PMC6904757]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2: WH320 specific growth rate 0.096 1/h; Glucose uptake 1.31 mmol/gBM/h; Acetate uptake 0.17 mmol/gBM/h.
growth_records.notes: Table 2. WH320 = DSM319 delta-lacZ. doubling_time=0.693/0.096=7.22 h. Uptake fluxes via Eq.(1).

up.method: calculated via Eq.(1) from experimental data

growth_records.cite: Aminian-Dehkordi J et al. Manually curated genome-scale reconstruction of the metabolic network of Bacillus megaterium DSM319. Scientific Reports (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41598-019-55041-w. [PMC6904757]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2: WH323 specific growth rate 0.107 1/h; Glucose uptake 1.53 mmol/gBM/h; Acetate uptake 0.16 mmol/gBM/h.
growth_records.notes: Table 2. WH323 = DSM319 XylA1-spoVG-lacZ. doubling_time=0.693/0.107=6.48 h. Uptake fluxes via Eq.(1).

phenotypes.base_medium: Phenotype-microarray / substrate-utilisation assay (defined minimal + sole substrate)

Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
Phenotype-microarray / substrate-utilisation assay (defined minimal + sole substrate)
Phenotype-microarray / substrate-utilisation assay (defined minimal + sole substrate)

Phenotype-microarray / substrate-utilisation assay (defined minimal + sole substrate)
Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
